input.counterSource: CSS-Tricks "Masonry Layout is Now grid-lanes" (https://css-tricks.com/masonry-layout-is-now-grid-lanes/) and WebKit blog "Introducing CSS Grid Lanes" (https://webkit.org/blog/17660/introducing-css-grid-lanes/) both confirm `display: grid-lanes` is the correct current syntax and that `grid-template-rows: masonry` was the prior experimental approach that was superseded. No credible source contradicts the claim.
input.evidence: The core factual assertions in the claim are accurate: (1) `display: grid-lanes` is the current CSS syntax for masonry layout as defined in CSS Grid Layout Level 3, confirmed by MDN, WebKit, CSS-Tricks, and the CSSWG drafts at drafts.csswg.org/css-grid-3/. (2) The old `grid-template-rows: masonry` approach was a Firefox-only experimental syntax that was dropped by the CSSWG in favor of `display: grid-lanes`, resolved in January 2025. (3) The "grid-lanes model" framing matches the specification's own terminology. One caveat: the supporting quote ("Level 3 of the CSS grid layout specification defines masonry layout (also known as grid-lanes layout)") establishes the layout's name but does not directly state the CSS property value is `display: grid-lanes` — the claim slightly overreads the quote by treating the layout model name as proof of the property syntax. However, this is a minor attribution issue, not a factual error. The underlying claim is well-supported by primary sources (MDN, W3C spec, WebKit blog, CSSWG GitHub). Safari 26 shipped `display: grid-lanes` first in 2026; Chrome and Firefox have it behind flags.
